title: heuchera parviflora var. rugelii
intro: Heuchera parviflora var. rugelii, also known as Coral Bells, is a variety of Heuchera plants that are native to the southeastern United States and are characterized by their maple-like leaves with wavy edges. The plant's leaves contain salicin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties, making it useful for medicinal purposes. The health benefits of Heuchera parviflora var. rugelii include reducing inflammation and relieving pain, as well as being used as a natural antiseptic and astringent. Heuchera parviflora var. rugelii can be prepared for medicinal use through infusions, decoctions, or salves, although it's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it for therapeutic purposes.
